commandsRun: python3 - <<'PY' ... socket checks for 127.0.0.1:3122 and 127.0.0.1:5037 ... PY

assertions.title: Connecting to a reachable SSH host opens an interactive shell on the intended target

steps.action: Open the shipped session screen with the saved SSH fixture host visible
steps.expected: Host row is visible and connect can be triggered from the real app shell
steps.observed: Visible session host row and connect control were exercised through MainActivity / AtermNavHost.

steps.action: Tap connect and accept the host-trust prompt
steps.expected: Connection transitions into a real connected session
steps.observed: The trust prompt appeared for 10.0.2.2:3122 and the session reached Connected.

steps.action: Inspect endpoint and transcript proof
steps.expected: Terminal proves it is attached to the intended remote fixture host
steps.observed: UI asserted session_active_endpoint=10.0.2.2:3122 and transcript contained ATERM_REMOTE_PROOF:10.0.2.2:3122.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted in the copied instrumentation logcat for this flow.
evidence.network: Real SSH fixture connection from emulator to 10.0.2.2:3122 reached CONNECTED and produced remote-only proof text.

assertions.title: A visible connecting state is shown while session setup is still in progress

steps.action: Tap connect on a deliberately delayed session flow
steps.expected: A visible in-progress state is shown before completion
steps.observed: The surface displayed Connecting… with a visible cancel/disconnect button and disabled repeat connect.

steps.action: Cancel while setup is still in progress
steps.expected: The pending state resolves cleanly instead of hanging or looking connected
steps.observed: The flow resolved to Connection canceled. with no trust prompt left behind and no completed connection.

steps.action: Compare with the real-fixture prompt phase
steps.expected: The real fixture also remains in a connecting/trust-gated state until resolved
steps.observed: The live-fixture flow remained in CONNECTING with a pending trust decision until the user decision was applied.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted in the copied instrumentation logcat for the connecting-state runs.
evidence.network: Delayed/scripted session handshake on 10.0.2.2:3130 showed Connecting… before cancel; live fixture prompt phase stayed connecting until trust resolution.

assertions.title: Authentication failure is surfaced clearly and leaves the app disconnected

steps.action: Attempt a connection with an invalid password against the fixture host
steps.expected: Authentication fails clearly and no shell opens
steps.observed: After trust acceptance, the flow ended with Authentication failed. Verify the linked identity and try again.

steps.action: Check the visible session surface
steps.expected: The user sees an explicit auth failure on the session screen
steps.observed: The session_status_message UI asserted Authentication failed. on the real session surface.

steps.action: Confirm session outcome
steps.expected: No usable terminal prompt is presented
steps.observed: The tests finished in FAILED/disconnected state rather than a connected shell.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted in copied logcat beyond the expected authentication failure state.
evidence.network: SSH auth attempt to 10.0.2.2:3122 failed after trust acceptance; UI surfaced explicit authentication failure.

assertions.title: DNS, timeout, and unreachable-host failures are surfaced clearly

steps.action: Exercise DNS failure and unreachable-host flows
steps.expected: Both failures surface clear disconnected-state messaging
steps.observed: DNS lookup failed for nonexistent.invalid:3122 and Host is unreachable at 10.0.2.2:65022 were both validated.

steps.action: Look for a timeout-specific runnable validation
steps.expected: A timeout path is also exercised through the session surface
steps.observed: No timeout-specific instrumentation flow was found or rerun for this assignment.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted for the DNS/unreachable runs.
evidence.network: Validated DNS failure and unreachable-host failure; timeout variant remains unproven in runnable user-surface evidence.

assertions.issues: Timeout-specific failure coverage was not present in the runnable validation subset, so the full contract assertion cannot be marked pass.

assertions.title: First-time connection to an unknown host key blocks on an explicit trust decision

steps.action: Initiate the first connection to the unknown SSH fixture host
steps.expected: Shell entry is blocked behind a visible trust prompt with fingerprint details
steps.observed: The session UI displayed session_trust_prompt for 10.0.2.2:3122 before any connected shell state.

steps.action: Reject the trust decision
steps.expected: The attempt aborts and no trust record is silently persisted
steps.observed: The live-fixture validation reported Host trust rejected. and known trusted-host count remained 0.

steps.action: Retry and accept trust
steps.expected: Connection proceeds only after explicit acceptance
steps.observed: After acceptance, the flow connected successfully and remote proof appeared.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted in copied logcat for the trust-decision flows.
evidence.network: First SSH contact to 10.0.2.2:3122 paused for explicit trust; rejecting aborted the attempt and accepting allowed the connection.

assertions.title: Accepting a host key suppresses repeat prompts for the same unchanged server key

steps.action: Accept the SSH fixture host key on first connect
steps.expected: Trust is stored for that unchanged endpoint
steps.observed: The first accepted connection stored exactly one trusted-host record.

steps.action: Disconnect and reconnect to the same unchanged fixture endpoint
steps.expected: No trust prompt is shown again
steps.observed: Reconnections completed with pendingTrustDecision absent and session_trust_prompt count asserted to 0.

steps.action: Recreate the activity and reconnect again
steps.expected: Trust reuse still works after relaunch
steps.observed: After activity recreate, the same host reconnected without another trust prompt.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted for trusted reconnect flows.
evidence.network: Accepted trust on 10.0.2.2:3122 was reused across reconnect and relaunch without another trust prompt.

assertions.title: A changed host key is flagged as a mismatch and never trusted silently

steps.action: Attempt a connection with a previously stored but incorrect host key
steps.expected: The mismatch is blocked with a clear warning
steps.observed: The flow ended with Host key changed for 10.0.2.2:3122. Connection blocked.

steps.action: Check the session-surface mismatch message
steps.expected: The user sees the mismatch instead of a silent shell entry
steps.observed: The session_status_message UI asserted Host key changed for 127.0.0.1:2223. Connection blocked. in the session screen test.

steps.action: Confirm no automatic trust carry-over
steps.expected: No silent continuation into a live shell occurs
steps.observed: The flows stayed blocked and never reported CONNECTED after the changed-key condition.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted beyond the expected changed-host-key block.
evidence.network: Changed host key on a previously trusted endpoint was surfaced as a blocking mismatch rather than silently trusted.

assertions.title: Trusted-host decisions stay bound to the actual endpoint after host edits

steps.action: Trust the original endpoint and disconnect
steps.expected: The original endpoint becomes trusted only for that specific address/port
steps.observed: Trust was stored for the original endpoint after an accepted connection.

steps.action: Edit/repoint the host endpoint and reconnect
steps.expected: The new endpoint is treated as a fresh trust context
steps.observed: A fresh trust prompt appeared for the repointed endpoint instead of silently reusing old trust.

steps.action: Reject the fresh trust decision
steps.expected: The edited endpoint remains untrusted and disconnected
steps.observed: Rejecting the new-endpoint prompt produced Host trust rejected. and no trusted-host entry was created for the new endpoint.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-linked app errors were noted for endpoint-repoint trust flows.
evidence.network: Trust accepted on the original endpoint was not silently reused after host address/port changes; the repointed endpoint required a fresh decision.

frictions.description: The first broad connectedDebugAndroidTest run that included the full SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest/SessionRealConnectionFlowsInstrumentedTest classes picked up an unrelated terminal-resize failure (outside the assigned assertion set), so I reran only the connect/trust-relevant methods to ground these statuses cleanly.
frictions.resolution: Scoped the rerun to nine assertion-relevant test methods; that focused rerun passed 9/9.

frictions.description: The current instrumentation harness emits XML/logcat artifacts but no per-step screenshot files, so UI evidence is report/logcat based rather than screenshot based.

blockers.description: Timeout-specific failure handling was not exercised by any runnable user-surface validation method in the available connect/trust subset.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Reviewed SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest, SessionRealConnectionFlowsInstrumentedTest, and the focused rerun results; only DNS and unreachable-host failure paths were actually covered.

summary: Validated 8 assigned connect/trust assertions on emulator-5554 using focused instrumentation. PASS: VAL-CONNECT-001, VAL-CONNECT-002, VAL-CONNECT-003, VAL-CONNECT-005, VAL-CONNECT-006, VAL-CONNECT-007, VAL-CONNECT-012. BLOCKED: VAL-CONNECT-004 because timeout-specific failure coverage was not present in runnable user-surface evidence.
